summary:
Design a data structure that supports adding integers one at a time and reporting the median of everything added so far. The median is the middle value of the sorted data; when the count is even it is the average of the two middle values.

Implement a MedianFinder class with addNum(num) to insert a value into the running data set, and findMedian() to return the current median as a number.

examples.input: MedianFinder(), addNum(1), addNum(2), findMedian(), addNum(3), findMedian()
examples.explanation: After adding 1 and 2 the sorted data is [1,2] and the median is the average 1.5. After adding 3 the data is [1,2,3] and the median is the middle value 2.
With a single value the median is that value.

constraints: findMedian is only called after at least one number has been added
At most 5 * 10^4 calls will be made to addNum and findMedian
